info.title: Causal AI MVP API
info.description: Generated from Terraform routes and Pydantic models in scripts/openapi_models.py.

post.description: Create a new KG session UUID. This UUID is reused by KG and CD endpoints to keep data and tasks in the same tenant/session scope.
Return a pre-signed URL for uploading KG source data before calling /kg/add/{uuid}.

post.summary: Register KG metadata and get kg_id
post.description: Register an uploaded KG file in the current session and return kg_id. The returned kg_id is used by TraCKR endpoints via source_kg_id or transferred_knowledge.kg_id.

Return a pre-signed URL for uploading CD input data before calling algorithm run endpoints such as /cd/lingam/run/{uuid}, /cd/trackr/run/{uuid}, or /cd/multica/run/{uuid}.

Supported upload content types:
- application/json (canonical dataset payload)
- text/csv (single dataset CSV; for trackr/lingam)
- application/zip (MultiCa: one or more .csv files in a ZIP)
Note: MultiCa accepts JSON or ZIP input; non-JSON MultiCa input must be ZIP.
Queue a LayeredLiNGAM run using uploaded CD input data in the same session (session is initialized by /kg/init). This endpoint does not require matching or transferred_knowledge.
Start column matching between uploaded target data and a source KG. Requires source_kg_id from /kg/add/{uuid} in the same session.
Persist or update target->source column mapping for TraCKR before run.

post.summary: Run TraCKR with transferred KG knowledge
post.description: Queue a TraCKR run. transferred_knowledge.kg_id must come from /kg/add/{uuid}, and transferred_knowledge.session_uuid must equal path {uuid}.

Start automatic column matching for MultiCa using uploaded data only (no KG dependency).

Input formats:
- JSON: {"data": [dataset1, dataset2, ...]}
- ZIP: each .csv member is parsed as one dataset, in ZIP member order.
If metadata is provided, metadata length must match dataset count.
Persist or update column mapping used by MultiCa run.

Queue a MultiCa run using uploaded CD input data in the same session (session is initialized by /kg/init). This endpoint does not accept transferred_knowledge.

Input formats:
- JSON: {"data": [dataset1, dataset2, ...]}
- ZIP: each .csv member is parsed as one dataset, in ZIP member order.
Note: MultiCa requires at least 2 datasets.

get.description: Query asynchronous CD task execution status by task_id. Typical status transitions are queued -> running -> succeeded|failed.

Return a pre-signed URL for downloading the final CD result JSON after task completion.

Frontend contract (top-level metadata):
- `uuid` (string): session UUID.
- `task_id` (string): CD task id.
- `mode` (string): one of `"lingam"`, `"trackr"`, `"multica"`.
- `status` (string): `"succeeded"` for successful artifact.
- `timestamp` (string): ISO8601 UTC completion time.

Common CD payload fields:
- `feature_names` (string[]): ordered feature names used by causal discovery.
- `adjacency_matrices` (number[][]): discovered causal adjacency matrix in industry orientation `cause=row, effect=col`.
  - `adjacency_matrices[i][j] != 0` means feature `feature_names[i]` causes feature `feature_names[j]`.
- `num_datasets` (number): number of datasets used in fitting.
- `num_variables` (number): number of variables/features in result.
- `training_data.columns` (string[]): one-hot/processed training column order.
- `training_data.data` (number[][]): processed training rows aligned to `columns`.
- `feature_metadata.feature_types` (object): variable type map (e.g., numeric/categorical).
- `feature_metadata.feature_categories` (object): category mapping for one-hot groups.

Mode-specific fields:
- `trackr`: includes `kg_id` and `matching_used`.
- `multica`: includes `matching_used`.
- `lingam`: does not include `kg_id` / `matching_used`.

Cross-service note:
- Knowledge Graph uploads also use `cause=row, effect=col`.
- Decision Analysis converts this internally to Cation's `effect=row, cause=col` representation at DA ingest.

Which example to use by flow:
- If your flow is LiNGAM (`/cd/lingam/run/{uuid}`), use the `lingam` example.
- If your flow is TraCKR (`/cd/trackr/match` -> `/cd/trackr/run/{uuid}`), use the `trackr` example.
- If your flow is MultiCa (`/cd/multica/match` -> `/cd/multica/run/{uuid}`), use the `multica` example.

Example (lingam mode):
```json
{
  "uuid": "<session_uuid>",
  "task_id": "<cd_task_id>",
  "mode": "lingam",
  "status": "succeeded",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-26T22:31:06.016699+00:00",
  "feature_names": ["age", "income", "status=active"],
  "adjacency_matrices": [
    [0.0, 0.3, 0.0],
    [0.0, 0.0, 0.5],
    [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]
  ],
  "num_datasets": 1,
  "num_variables": 3,
  "training_data": {
    "columns": ["age", "income", "status=active"],
    "data": [[30.0, 60000.0, 1.0], [42.0, 85000.0, 0.0]]
  },
  "feature_metadata": {
    "feature_types": {
      "age": "numeric",
      "income": "numeric",
      "status=active": "one_hot"
    },
    "feature_categories": {
      "status": ["status=active"]
    }
  }
}
```

Example (trackr mode):
```json
{
  "uuid": "<session_uuid>",
  "task_id": "<cd_task_id>",
  "mode": "trackr",
  "status": "succeeded",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-26T22:31:06.016699+00:00",
  "feature_names": ["age", "income", "status=active"],
  "adjacency_matrices": [
    [0.0, 0.2, 0.0],
    [0.1, 0.0, 0.4],
    [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]
  ],
  "num_datasets": 1,
  "num_variables": 3,
  "kg_id": "<kg_id>",
  "matching_used": "matching/trackr/<session_uuid>/state.json",
  "training_data": {
    "columns": ["age", "income", "status=active"],
    "data": [[30.0, 60000.0, 1.0], [42.0, 85000.0, 0.0]]
  },
  "feature_metadata": {
    "feature_types": {
      "age": "numeric",
      "income": "numeric",
      "status=active": "one_hot"
    },
    "feature_categories": {
      "status": ["status=active"]
    }
  }
}
```

Example (multica mode):
```json
{
  "uuid": "<session_uuid>",
  "task_id": "<cd_task_id>",
  "mode": "multica",
  "status": "succeeded",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-26T22:31:06.016699+00:00",
  "feature_names": ["region=APAC", "budget", "conversion"],
  "adjacency_matrices": [
    [0.0, 0.15, 0.10],
    [0.0, 0.0, 0.45],
    [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]
  ],
  "num_datasets": 3,
  "num_variables": 3,
  "matching_used": "matching/multica/<session_uuid>/state.json",
  "training_data": {
    "columns": ["region=APAC", "budget", "conversion"],
    "data": [[1.0, 1200.0, 0.3], [0.0, 1500.0, 0.4]]
  },
  "feature_metadata": {
    "feature_types": {
      "region=APAC": "one_hot",
      "budget": "numeric",
      "conversion": "numeric"
    },
    "feature_categories": {
      "region": ["region=APAC"]
    }
  }
}
```

post.description: Queue a DA explain task using CD result reference (cd_result_reference.session_uuid + task_id). Typical usage is LiNGAM/CD completion followed by DA explain.
Queue a DA enumerate task using CD result reference. Use this when you need multiple candidate actions/options.

get.description: Query asynchronous DA task execution status by task_id. Typical status transitions are queued -> running -> succeeded|failed.

Return a pre-signed URL for downloading the final DA result JSON after task completion.

Frontend contract (top-level metadata):
- `uuid` (string): session UUID.
- `task_id` (string): DA task id.
- `mode` (string): `"explain"` or `"enumerate"`.
- `status` (string): `"succeeded"` for successful artifact.
- `timestamp` (string): ISO8601 UTC completion time.

Mode-specific payload:
- `mode="explain"`: response has `result` (object).
- `mode="enumerate"`: response has `results` (array of explain-like objects).

Explain result object (full fields):
- `is_solved` (boolean): whether thresholds are satisfied by a found action.
- `z` (number[]): current full feature vector.
- `x` (number[]): current actionable-feature sub-vector.
- `y` (number[]): current target values.
- `threshold` (number[]): requested target thresholds.
- `action` (number[]): recommended feature deltas.
- `effect` (number[][]): per-graph causal effect on all features.
- `z_cf` (number[][]): per-graph counterfactual full feature vectors.
- `x_cf` (number[][]): per-graph counterfactual actionable vectors.
- `y_cf` (number[][]): per-graph counterfactual target values.
- `cost` (number): optimization cost.
- `dist` (number[]): per-graph distance/penalty terms.
- `obj` (number): optimization objective value.
- `support` (number[]): indices of non-zero action entries.
- `support_effect` (number[]): indices where causal effect exceeds tolerance.
- `time` (number): solver elapsed time in seconds.

Unsolved behavior (`is_solved=false`):
- `action` is all zeros.
- `cost` is `0.0`.
- `dist` is all zeros.
- `obj` is large (`1e13`, MAX_VAL).
- `z_cf` repeats current `z`; `y_cf` mirrors current targets per graph.

Note for frontend:
- In `enumerate` mode, `results` can be an empty array when no feasible non-empty action is found.

Example (explain mode, unsolved):
```json
{
  "uuid": "<session_uuid>",
  "task_id": "<da_task_id>",
  "mode": "explain",
  "status": "succeeded",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-26T22:31:06.016699+00:00",
  "result": {
    "is_solved": false,
    "z": [0.5, 1.2, 2.1],
    "x": [0.5, 1.2],
    "y": [0.5],
    "threshold": [0.8],
    "action": [0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    "effect": [[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]],
    "z_cf": [[0.5, 1.2, 2.1]],
    "x_cf": [[0.5, 1.2]],
    "y_cf": [[0.5]],
    "cost": 0.0,
    "dist": [0.0],
    "obj": 10000000000000.0,
    "support": [],
    "support_effect": [],
    "time": 0.0021
  }
}
```

Example (enumerate mode):
```json
{
  "uuid": "<session_uuid>",
  "task_id": "<da_task_id>",
  "mode": "enumerate",
  "status": "succeeded",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-26T22:31:06.016699+00:00",
  "results": [
    {
      "is_solved": true,
      "z": [0.5, 1.2, 2.1],
      "x": [0.5, 1.2],
      "y": [0.5],
      "threshold": [0.8],
      "action": [0.3, 0.0, 0.1],
      "effect": [[0.02, 0.01, 0.00]],
      "z_cf": [[0.82, 1.21, 2.2]],
      "x_cf": [[0.82, 1.21]],
      "y_cf": [[0.81]],
      "cost": 0.4,
      "dist": [0.0],
      "obj": 0.4,
      "support": [0, 2],
      "support_effect": [0, 1],
      "time": 0.0049
    }
  ]
}
```

Schemas by mode: [DAExplainArtifact](#/components/schemas/DAExplainArtifact), [DAEnumerateArtifact](#/components/schemas/DAEnumerateArtifact).

delete.description: Delete DA task metadata and related artifacts by task_id in the given session.

post.description: Create a new conversation session for the agent wizard flow.

post.summary: Send user chat message to agent
post.description: Send chat messages to the in-process agent runtime and return the next assistant response.

post.summary: Approve or reject deferred agent action
post.description: Resume a paused agent turn after a user approves or rejects an action card.

post.summary: Advance or query current wizard step
post.description: Advance to the next step when possible, or ask the agent what is still required.

get.description: Return the latest persisted state for a given agent session identifier.
Return KG detail and downloadable payload metadata for a selected graph.
List all knowledge graphs available under the current session UUID.

tags.name: CD Common 01 Upload
tags.description: Generate upload URLs for CD inputs

tags.name: CD Common 07 Delete
tags.description: Delete CD tasks or matching state

tags.name: KG 02 Upload
tags.description: Generate upload URLs for KG inputs

tags.name: Flow: TraCKR + KG
tags.description: Cross-service flow: KG init/upload/add -> TraCKR match/set/run -> status/result.

tags.name: Flow: MultiCa
tags.description: Session flow: KG init (session bootstrap) -> CD upload -> MultiCa match/set/run -> status/result.

tags.name: Flow: LiNGAM
tags.description: Session flow: KG init (session bootstrap) -> CD upload -> LiNGAM run -> status/result.

tags.name: Flow: CD + DA
tags.description: Decision analysis flow continues from causal discovery results: KG init -> CD upload -> CD run (LiNGAM/TraCKR/MultiCa) -> CD result -> DA explain/enumerate -> DA status/result.

tags.name: DA 01 Explain
tags.description: Submit DA explain tasks from CD results

tags.name: DA 02 Enumerate
tags.description: Submit DA enumerate tasks from CD results

tags.name: Agent 01 Chat
tags.description: Send user messages into agent flow

tags.name: Agent 02 Approve
tags.description: Approve or reject deferred agent actions

tags.name: Agent 03 Next
tags.description: Advance or query current wizard step

tags.name: Flow: Agent Wizard
tags.description: Agent workflow: create session -> chat -> approve deferred actions -> advance next step.

KGDeleteResponse.description: Response payload after deleting a KG session and all graphs.

AgentMessageRequest.description: Single message item for chat requests.

AgentPassthroughResponse.description: Generic response envelope from in-process agent runtime.

DAExplainArtifact.description: DA explain artifact saved by worker and downloaded from `result_url`. Top-level metadata wraps an algorithm-specific `result` object.

is_solved.description: Whether thresholds are satisfied by found action.

action.description: Recommended feature deltas (all zeros when no action found).

effect.description: Estimated effect on targets for each candidate action.

obj.description: Objective value; very large often means unsolved.

DAEnumerateArtifact.description: DA enumerate artifact saved by worker and downloaded from `result_url`. Top-level metadata wraps a list of explain-like `results`.

results.description: List of candidate actions; may be empty if no feasible action.

is_solved.description: Whether thresholds are satisfied by found action.

action.description: Recommended feature deltas (all zeros when no action found).

effect.description: Estimated effect on targets for each candidate action.

obj.description: Objective value; very large often means unsolved.
